title: Protests by the youth for ‘system change  have not had much impact  so far except for the changing of one set of  ministerial crooks for another set of crooks while the political establishment consolidates itself!
body_lines: When questioned as to why Sri Lanka’s law works to its most ferocious extent against anti-government protestors, Premier Ranil Wickremesinghe’s answer was typically slick.

‘The police have also arrested Members of Parliament…it is the magistrates who decide on remand’, he said. This was during one of multiple regional and global media interviews indulged in freely as the nation’s citizenry struggles in the death throes of decades of gross unchecked political corruption by Presidents and Prime Ministers of all shades and sizes.

Meanwhile allegations abound that citizens who have not committed violence are being hauled in for questioning, the latest being a Catholic nun. Premier Wickremesinghe peppered his remarks with references to the Bar Association’s role in working with state agencies to prevent abuse.

In passing, it must also be said that the Prime Minister’s co-opting of the Bar’s statements to bolster ‘his’ Government’s various positions, including the departure of the President which is a core demand of the protestors, is becoming uncomfortably frequent, to say the least.

The Bar’s denial, through a statement issued by its Executive Committee on Thursday, of the Prime Minister’s reported assertion that it ‘agreed’ to a quid-pro-quo with President Gotabhaya Rajapaksa that he could remain if the Executive Presidency is abolished, is a case in point.

The President himself asserted recently that he will continue in office and that he cannot go out as a ‘failed President.’ This testifies to the fact that the pressure which erupted as a result of the country-wide protests have now been, in  the Government’s thinking at least, ‘managed’ with the well planned advent of Premier Wickremesinghe. The President and the Prime Minister may both be nastily wrong in that assessment, despite a present lull as it were. From village to city, Sri Lankans are growing increasingly desperate as families starve.

With desperation comes fury as evidenced on May 9th apart from the fact that some attacks on offices and residences of politicians were well orchestrated by intra-party rivalries and other hidden sinister hands. Renewed violence may well be far worse than this, the result of a political leadership that goes on its merry way with scarce a thought for the consequences, even when we are well and truly in the abyss.  But to return to the Prime Ministerial explanation in regard to the unequal working of the law in regard to the present turmoil, this is both disingenuous and plainly incorrect.

The alarming lack of balance with which the law operates against pro-Government and anti-Government supporters is clear. This week, officers of the Attorney General’s Department submitted to court that former Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa and ‘several others’ including two former Ministers have not yet submitted their passports to court following a travel ban imposed on them in connection with instigation of the May 9th assaults on peaceful protestors at Galle face Green.  Former Minister Johnston Fernando was hiding in ‘plain sight’ of the law as they say, for several weeks before he surrendered.

On the other hand, youth protestors are liberally arrested or questioned by the police with a view to intimidation. That is the inequality of the law that the young interviewer questioning the Prime Minister was focussing on.

The clever parry and thrust of the answers that she received did not detract from that stark truth. In fact, this partiality and discrimination reflected in police arrests of protestors was pointed to in a recent letter of the Bar Association addressed to the Inspector General of Police (IGP), drawing serious concern to arrests of citizens being carried out solely on lists submitted by politicians.

Among other allegations of partiality was the use of lapsed Emergency Regulations to keep persons in custody. Rightly, the admission by the IGP himself that the command structure of the police has broken down due to political appointments to the police force, was emphasized. That inequality continues to be manifested at various other levels.
On Monday leading headlines in international news media announced the conviction of former Minister Prasanna Ranatunga on charges of extorting money from a businessman.

For two coruscating news reports on this, see ‘Sri Lanka’s urban development minister convicted of extortion’, Straits Times, June 6th and ‘Sri Lankan cricketer Ranatunga’s brother sentenced to two years imprisonment for extortion’ Times of India carrying a Press Trust of India story of the same date).

Commensurate with that, we have the Government quick-fire response to the effect that the conviction will not affect this worthy’s parliamentary seat or his ministerial portfolio. True, Article 91 read with Article 89 of the Constitution does not encompass parliamentarians on whom suspended sentences have been imposed, within its legal remit.

Incidentally, it may be high time that we look at amendment of these constitutional provisions to take out the condition that the disqualification to be elected/serve in Parliament applies only to those who are ordered to serve the sentence.

Quite apart from that, the legal rationale for imposing a suspended sentence on an offender goes to an assessment that criminal behaviour will not be repeated. Is that a given with our politicians, we may well ask?
Regardless, even more than the law, the issue is the chutzpah with which the Government greets the fact that a criminal continues not only to be in Parliament but holds his portfolio.
So when the President and the Prime Minister beg the world to give money to bail the Government out of the mess that is wholly their own doing, do they not recognize the grand irony thereto?
Or as is probably the case, do they simply stake their begging on one more toss of the political coin?

If so, they must be educated that world opinion links these developments in no uncertain terms. For example, many global news reports looked at the conviction of this Minister in the context of the country’s financial meltdown. How much more do we have to fall down the Rabbit Hole for the political leadership to understand this?

At least, should not the (Ministerial) convict be stripped of his portfolio? That he has appealed against the conviction does not make an iota of a difference. Rogues at high places and rogues at low places continues to bedevil Sri Lanka. The latest is a financial ‘high flyer’ known for messy public controversies but approved to be ‘parachuted’ to Parliament through the National List in record quick time. Is this what the Government parades as a nod for month long protests by the youth for ‘system change’?

These protests have not had impact except for the changing of ministerial crooks for another set of crooks while the political establishment consolidates itself. Even basic foundations of institutional independence are discarded. A classic example was when President Rajapaksa assured the Governor of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) some weeks ago that he will not ‘interfere’ with the CBSL.

But the autonomy and independence of the CBSL and the Monetary Board is not a gift to be granted on Presidential magnanimity. As with all institutions, independence must be secured by law as well as acted upon in practice by men and women of integrity and expertise. Sri Lanka has spectacularly failed in that regard. Political sycophants in these positions have led Sri Lanka to the crisis that is now upon us.

si.title: දේශපාලන සංස්ථාපිතය තහවුරු වෙද්දී එක ඇමති තක්කඩි සෙට් එකක් තවත් තක්කඩි සෙට් එකක් වෙනුවෙන් මාරු වෙනවා මිසක් ‘ක්‍රම මාරුවක්’ වෙනුවෙන් තාරුණ්‍යයේ විරෝධතා මේ වන තෙක් එතරම් බලපෑමක් වී නැත!
body_lines: රාජ්‍ය විරෝධී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට එරෙහිව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ නීතිය වඩාත් දරුණු ලෙස ක්‍රියාත්මක වන්නේ මන්දැයි ප්‍රශ්න කළ විට, අගමැති රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහගේ පිළිතුර සාමාන්‍යයෙන් සිනිඳු විය.

‘පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරුන්වත් පොලිසිය විසින් අත්අඩංගුවට ගෙන තිබෙනවා... රිමාන්ඩ් කිරීම තීරණය කරන්නේ මහේස්ත්‍රාත්වරුන්’ යැයි ඔහු පැවසීය. මෙය සිදු වූයේ, විවිධ ප්‍රමාණයේ ජනාධිපතිවරුන් සහ අගමැතිවරුන් විසින් දශක ගණනාවක් තිස්සේ පාලනය නොකළ දළ දේශපාලන දූෂණයේ මරණ මංචකයේ ජාතියේ පුරවැසියන් අරගල කරන විට නිදහසේ නියැළුණු කලාපීය සහ ගෝලීය මාධ්‍ය සම්මුඛ සාකච්ඡා කිහිපයකින් එකකි.

මේ අතර ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය සිදු නොකළ පුරවැසියන් ප්‍රශ්න කිරීම සඳහා ගෙන්වා ගන්නා බවට චෝදනා නැඟෙන අතර, අලුත්ම තැනැත්තා කතෝලික කන්‍යා සොහොයුරියකි. අග‍්‍රාමාත්‍ය වික‍්‍රමසිංහ මහතා අපයෝජනය වැලැක්වීම සඳහා රාජ්‍ය ආයතන සමග එක්ව කටයුතු කිරීමේදී නීතිඥ සංගමයේ කාර්ය භාරය ගැන සඳහන් කරමින් සිය ප‍්‍රකාශය කර ඇත.

එය සම්මත කිරීමේදී, විරෝධතාකරුවන්ගේ මූලික ඉල්ලීමක් වන ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ ඉවත්වීම ඇතුළු ‘ඔහුගේ’ ආණ්ඩුවේ විවිධ තනතුරු තර කර ගැනීම සඳහා නීතීඥ ප්‍රකාශවලට අගමැතිතුමා සහය වීම නිතර නිතර අසහනකාරී වෙමින් පවතින බව කිව යුතුය. අවම වශයෙන්.

විධායක ජනාධිපති ක‍්‍රමය අහෝසි කළහොත් තමාට තවදුරටත් සිටිය හැකි බවට ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්‍ෂ මහතා සමග කළ ප‍්‍රකට ප‍්‍රකාශයකට ‘එකඟ වූ’ බවට අගමැතිවරයා විසින් වාර්තා කරන ලද ප‍්‍රකාශය බ්‍රහස්පතින්දා එහි විධායක කමිටුව විසින් නිකුත් කරන ලද ප‍්‍රකාශයක් හරහා නීතිඥ මණ්ඩලය ප‍්‍රතික්ෂේප කිරීම, කාරණය සම්බන්ධයෙන්.

තමා තවදුරටත් ධුරයේ කටයුතු කරන බවත් අසාර්ථක ජනාධිපතිවරයකු ලෙස තමාට එළියට යා නොහැකි බවත් ජනාධිපතිවරයාම පසුගියදා ප්‍රකාශ කළේය. රට පුරා ඇති වූ විරෝධතාවල ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස මතු වූ පීඩනය මේ වන විට රජය තුළ ඇති වී ඇති බවට මෙයින් සාක්ෂි දරයි. අඩුම තරමින් අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහගේ මනා සැලසුම් සහගත ආගමනයත් සමඟ 'කළමනාකරණය' කර ඇත. වත්මන් නිහැඬියාවක් වුවද එම තක්සේරුවේදී ජනාධිපතිවරයා සහ අගමැතිවරයා යන දෙදෙනාම නරක ලෙස වැරදි විය හැකිය. ගමෙන් නගරයට, පවුල් සාගින්නෙන් පෙළෙන බැවින් ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් වඩ වඩාත් මංමුලා සහගතව වැඩෙමින් තිබේ.

දේශපාලකයන්ගේ කාර්යාල සහ නිවාසවලට එල්ල වූ ඇතැම් ප්‍රහාර අභ්‍යන්තර පක්ෂ එදිරිවාදිකම් සහ වෙනත් සැඟවුණු පව්කාර හස්තයන් විසින් මනාව මෙහෙයවා ඇති බව හැරුණු විට මැයි 9 වැනි දින පෙන්නුම් කරන පරිදි මංමුලා සහගතව කෝපයට පත් වේ. අප යලිත් ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය මීට වඩා බෙහෙවින් නරක විය හැකිය, අප හොඳින් හා සැබවින්ම අගාධයේ සිටින විට පවා, ප්‍රතිවිපාක ගැන සිතුවිල්ලක් නොමැතිව විනෝදජනක ගමනක් යන දේශපාලන නායකත්වයක ප්‍රතිඵලය. එහෙත් වත්මන් ව්‍යාකූලත්වය සම්බන්ධයෙන් නීතියේ අසමාන ක්‍රියාකාරිත්වය සම්බන්ධයෙන් අග්‍රාමාත්‍යවරයාගේ පැහැදිලි කිරීම වෙත ආපසු යාම, මෙය සාවද්‍ය මෙන්ම පැහැදිලිවම වැරදිය.

රජයට ගැති සහ රාජ්‍ය විරෝධී ආධාරකරුවන්ට එරෙහිව නීතිය ක්‍රියාත්මක වන භයානක සමතුලිතතාවයක් නොමැතිකම පැහැදිලිය. මැයි 9 වැනිදා උසිගැන්වීම්වලට අදාළව හිටපු අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්‍ෂ සහ හිටපු ඇමැතිවරුන් දෙදෙනකු ඇතුළු ‘කීපදෙනකුගේ’ විදේශ ගමන් බලපත්‍ර අධිකරණයට මෙතෙක් ඉදිරිපත් කර නොමැති බවට නීතිපති දෙපාර්තමේන්තුවේ නිලධාරීන් මේ සතියේ අධිකරණයට කරුණු ඉදිරිපත් කළේය. ගාලු මුවදොර දී සාමකාමී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට පහර දීම. හිටපු අමාත්‍ය ජොන්ස්ටන් ප්‍රනාන්දු යටත් වීමට පෙර සති කිහිපයක්ම ඔවුන් පවසන පරිදි නීතියේ ‘පැහැදිලි දැක්මේ’ සැඟවී සිටියේය.

අනෙක් අතට, තරුණ විරෝධතාකරුවන් පොලිසිය විසින් නොමසුරුව අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම හෝ ප්‍රශ්න කිරීම බිය ගැන්වීමේ අරමුණින් සිදු කරයි. අගමැතිවරයාගෙන් ප්‍රශ්න කරන තරුණ සම්මුඛ පරීක්ෂකවරයා අවධානය යොමු කළ නීතියේ අසමානතාවය එයයි.

ඇයට ලැබුණු පිළිතුරුවල ඇති දක්‍ෂ පරික්‍ෂණ සහ තෙරපුම එම අමූලික සත්‍යයට බාධාවක් නොවීය. ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම, විරෝධතාකරුවන් පොලිස් අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම් තුළ පිළිබිඹු වන මෙම පක්ෂග්‍රාහීත්වය සහ වෙනස් කොට සැලකීම, ඉදිරිපත් කරන ලද ලැයිස්තු මත පමණක් සිදු කෙරෙන පුරවැසියන් අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම් සම්බන්ධයෙන් දැඩි අවධානයක් යොමු කරමින්, නීතීඥ සංගමය විසින් පොලිස්පති (IGP) වෙත යොමු කරන ලද මෑත ලිපියක පෙන්වා දී ඇත. දේශපාලනඥයන්.

පක්ෂග්‍රාහීභාවය පිළිබඳ අනෙකුත් චෝදනා අතර පුද්ගලයන් අත්අඩංගුවේ තබා ගැනීම සඳහා අවලංගු වූ හදිසි නීති රෙගුලාසි භාවිතා කිරීම ද විය. පොලිසියට දේශපාලන පත්වීම් ලබාදීම නිසා පොලිසියේ අණදෙන ව්‍යුහය බිඳ වැටී ඇති බව පොලිස්පතිවරයා විසින්ම පිළිගැනීම නිවැරැදිව මෙහිදී අවධාරණය කෙරිණි. එම අසමානතාවය තවත් විවිධ මට්ටම්වලින් දිගින් දිගටම ප්‍රකාශ වේ.
සඳුදා දින ජාත්‍යන්තර ප්‍රවෘත්ති මාධ්‍යවල ප්‍රමුඛ සිරස්තල මගින් ප්‍රකාශ කළේ හිටපු අමාත්‍ය ප්‍රසන්න රණතුංග ව්‍යාපාරිකයෙකුගෙන් කප්පම් ගැනීමේ චෝදනාවට වරදකරු කර ඇති බවයි.

මේ පිළිබඳ විචිත්‍රවත් ප්‍රවෘත්ති දෙකක් සඳහා, 'ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ නාගරික සංවර්ධන ඇමති කප්පම් ගැනීමකට වරදකරු', ස්ට්‍රේට් ටයිම්ස්, ජුනි 6 සහ 'ශ්‍රී ලංකා ක්‍රිකට් ක්‍රීඩක රණතුංගගේ සොහොයුරාට කප්පම් ගැනීම් සම්බන්ධයෙන් වසර දෙකක සිර දඬුවමක්' බලන්න ටයිම්ස් ඔෆ් ඉන්දියා ප්‍රෙස් ට්‍රස්ට් ඔෆ් ඉන්දියා ප්‍රවෘත්තිය රැගෙන එම දිනයේම).

ඊට සරිලන පරිදි, මෙම වරදකරුගේ පාර්ලිමේන්තු අසුනට හෝ ඔහුගේ අමාත්‍ය ධූරයට එම වරද බල නොපාන බවට ආණ්ඩුවේ ඉක්මන් ප්‍රතිචාරය අප සතුව ඇත. ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 89 වැනි වගන්තිය සමඟ කියවා ඇති 91 වැනි වගන්තිය එහි නීතිමය සීමාව තුළ අත්හිටවූ සිර දඬුවම් නියම කර ඇති පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරුන් ඇතුළත් නොවන බව සත්‍යයකි.

අහඹු ලෙස, පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ තේරී පත්වීමට / සේවය කිරීමට නුසුදුසුකම අදාළ වන්නේ දඩුවම් විඳීමට නියම වූවන්ට පමණක් යන කොන්දේසිය ඉවත් කිරීම සඳහා මෙම ව්‍යවස්ථාමය ප්‍රතිපාදන සංශෝධනය කිරීම දෙස බැලීමට කාලය එළඹිය හැකිය.
ඊට අමතරව, වැරදිකරුවෙකුට අත්හිටවූ සිරදඬුවමක් පැනවීමේ නීතිමය තාර්කික සාපරාධී හැසිරීම නැවත සිදු නොවන බවට තක්සේරුවකට යයි. එය අපේ දේශපාලකයන් සමඟ ලබා දී ඇති දෙයක්ද, අපි හොඳින් ඇසිය හැකිය.
නීතිය කුමක් වුවත්, ප්‍රශ්නය වන්නේ අපරාධකරුවෙකු පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ සිටීම පමණක් නොව ඔහුගේ අමාත්‍යධුරය ද දරමින් සිටීම ගැන රජය ආචාර කරන චූට්ටායි.
එසේ නම් ජනාධිපතිවරයා සහ අගමැතිවරයා සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම තමන්ගේම ක්‍රියාවක් වන මේ අවුලෙන් රජයට ඇප දීමට මුදල් දෙන ලෙස ලෝකයෙන් බැගෑපත්ව ඉල්ලා සිටින විට එහි ඇති මහා උත්ප්‍රාසය ඔවුන් හඳුනා නොගන්නේද?
එසේත් නැතිනම් බොහෝ විට සිදු වන පරිදි, ඔවුන් හුදෙක් දේශපාලන කාසියේ තවත් එක් කාසියක් මත ඔවුන්ගේ හිඟාකෑම පටවනවාද?

එසේ නම්, ලෝක මතය මෙම වර්ධනයන් අවිනිශ්චිත ලෙස සම්බන්ධ කරන බව ඔවුන් දැනුවත් කළ යුතුය. නිදසුනක් වශයෙන්, බොහෝ ගෝලීය ප්‍රවෘත්ති වාර්තා රටේ මූල්‍ය බිඳවැටීමේ සන්දර්භය තුළ මෙම ඇමතිවරයාගේ වරදකරු බවට පත් වීම දෙස බලයි. දේශපාලන නායකත්වයට මේක තේරුම් ගන්න අපි තව කොච්චර හාවුන් හිලෙන් වැටෙන්න ඕනෙද?

අඩුම තරමින්, (ඇමති) වැරදිකරුගේ අමාත්‍ය ධූරයෙන් වත් ඉවත් කළ යුතු නොවේද? ඔහු එම වරදට එරෙහිව අභියාචනයක් ඉදිරිපත් කර තිබීමෙන් සුළු වෙනසක් සිදු නොවේ. උස් තැන්වල සිටින තක්කඩියන් සහ පහත් තැන්වල සිටින තක්කඩියන් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තවදුරටත් යකා කරයි. අලුත්ම එක, අවුල් සහගත මහජන මතභේද සඳහා ප්‍රසිද්ධ මූල්‍ය ‘ඉහළ පියාසර කරන්නෙකු’ වන නමුත් වාර්තාගත ඉක්මන් කාලයකදී ජාතික ලැයිස්තුව හරහා පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට ‘පැරෂුට්’ කිරීමට අනුමැතිය ලබා දී ඇත. ‘ක්‍රමය වෙනස් කිරීම’ සඳහා තරුණයින් විසින් මාසයක් පුරා කරන විරෝධතාවලට ආණ්ඩුව හිස නමා පෙළපාළි යන්නේ මෙයද?

දේශපාලන සංස්ථාපිතය ශක්තිමත් වන විට තවත් තක්කඩි සෙට් එකකට ඇමැති තක්කඩි මාරු කළා මිස මේ විරෝධතාවලින් බලපෑමක් නැත. ආයතනික ස්වාධීනත්වයේ මූලික පදනම් පවා ඉවත දමනු ලැබේ. සම්භාව්‍ය නිදසුන නම්, ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුවට ‘ඇගිලි ගැසීම්’ නොකරන බවට ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂ මහතා සති කිහිපයකට පෙර ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුවේ (CBSL) අධිපතිවරයාට සහතික වීම ය.

එහෙත් ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුවේ සහ මුදල් මණ්ඩලයේ ස්වාධීනත්වය සහ ස්වාධීනත්වය ජනාධිපතිගේ උදාරත්වය මත පිරිනැමිය යුතු තෑග්ගක් නොවේ. සියලුම ආයතනවල මෙන්, ස්වාධීනත්වය නීතියෙන් මෙන්ම ප්‍රායෝගිකව ක්‍රියා කළ යුත්තේ ඒකාග්‍රතාවයෙන් හා ප්‍රවීණත්වයෙන් යුතු පිරිමින් සහ කාන්තාවන් විසිනි. ඒ අතින් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව පුදුමාකාර ලෙස අසාර්ථක වී ඇත. මෙම තනතුරුවල සිටින දේශපාලන ගෝලබාලයන් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව දැන් අප හමුවේ ඇති අර්බුදයට ගෙන ගොස් ඇත.

ta.title: ‘அரசியல் ஸ்தாபனமே தன்னை நிலைநிறுத்திக் கொள்ளும் வேளையில், ஒரு அமைச்சுக் குண்டர்களை இன்னொரு கும்பல் காக்க மாற்றியதைத் தவிர, ‘அமைப்பு மாற்றத்துக்காக இளைஞர்கள் நடத்திய போராட்டங்கள் இதுவரை பெரிய தாக்கத்தை ஏற்படுத்தவில்லை!
body_lines: அரசாங்கத்திற்கு எதிரான போராட்டக்காரர்களுக்கு எதிராக இலங்கையின் சட்டம் ஏன் மிகவும் மூர்க்கமான அளவிற்கு செயல்படுகிறது என்று கேள்வி எழுப்பப்பட்டபோது, ​​பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கவின் பதில் பொதுவாக மென்மையாய் இருந்தது.

‘நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர்களையும் போலீசார் கைது செய்துள்ளனர்... காவலில் வைப்பது குறித்து நீதிபதிகள்தான் முடிவு செய்வார்கள்’ என்றார். பல தசாப்தங்களாக ஜனாதிபதிகள் மற்றும் பிரதமர்கள் அனைத்து நிழல்கள் மற்றும் அளவுகள் கொண்ட கடுமையான அரசியல் ஊழலின் மரண வேதனையில் தேசத்தின் குடிமக்கள் போராடுகையில், சுதந்திரமாக ஈடுபட்டுள்ள பல பிராந்திய மற்றும் உலகளாவிய ஊடக நேர்காணல்களில் இதுவும் ஒன்றாகும்.

இதற்கிடையில், வன்முறையில் ஈடுபடாத குடிமக்கள் விசாரணைக்காக இழுத்துச் செல்லப்படுவதாக குற்றச்சாட்டுகள் ஏராளமாக உள்ளன, சமீபத்தியவர் ஒரு கத்தோலிக்க கன்னியாஸ்திரி. துஷ்பிரயோகத்தைத் தடுப்பதற்கு அரச நிறுவனங்களுடன் இணைந்து செயற்படுவதில் சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கத்தின் பங்கு பற்றிய குறிப்புகளுடன் பிரதமர் விக்கிரமசிங்க தனது கருத்துக்களைக் குறிப்பிட்டுள்ளார்.

இதற்கிடையில், எதிர்ப்பாளர்களின் முக்கிய கோரிக்கையான ஜனாதிபதி வெளியேறுவது உட்பட, 'அவரது' அரசாங்கத்தின் பல்வேறு நிலைகளை வலுப்படுத்த, வழக்கறிஞர்களின் அறிக்கைகளுக்கு பிரதமர் ஒத்துழைப்பது அடிக்கடி சங்கடமாகி வருகிறது என்பதையும் சொல்ல வேண்டும். குறைந்தது.

நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறை ஒழிக்கப்பட்டால், ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷவுடன் தாம் நீடிக்கலாம் என்ற க்விட்-ப்ரோ-கோவுக்கு 'ஒப்புக் கொண்டதாக' பிரதமரின் அறிக்கையின் அறிக்கையை, வியாழன் அன்று அதன் செயற்குழு வெளியிட்ட அறிக்கையின் மூலம், பட்டிமன்றம் மறுத்துள்ளது. வழக்கு.

தான் பதவியில் நீடிப்பேன் என்றும், 'தோல்வியடைந்த ஜனாதிபதியாக' வெளியேற முடியாது என்றும், ஜனாதிபதியே சமீபத்தில் உறுதியளித்தார். இது, நாடு தழுவிய போராட்டங்களின் விளைவாக வெடித்த அழுத்தம், இப்போது அரசாங்கத்தில் உள்ளது என்பதற்கு சாட்சியமளிக்கிறது. குறைந்தபட்சம், பிரதமர் விக்கிரமசிங்கவின் நன்கு திட்டமிடப்பட்ட வருகையுடன் 'நிர்வகிக்கப்பட்டதாக' நினைத்தேன். ஜனாதிபதி மற்றும் பிரதமர் இருவருமே அந்த மதிப்பீட்டில் மிகவும் தவறாக இருக்கலாம். கிராமம் முதல் நகரம் வரை, குடும்பங்கள் பட்டினியால் வாடுவதால், இலங்கையர்கள் பெருகிய முறையில் அவநம்பிக்கையடைந்து வருகின்றனர்.

அரசியல்வாதிகளின் அலுவலகங்கள் மற்றும் குடியிருப்புகள் மீதான சில தாக்குதல்கள் உள்கட்சி போட்டிகள் மற்றும் பிற மறைமுகமான கெட்ட கைகளால் நன்கு திட்டமிடப்பட்டவை என்பதைத் தவிர, மே 9 ஆம் தேதி விரக்தியுடன் கோபம் வருகிறது. புதுப்பிக்கப்பட்ட வன்முறை இதைவிட மிக மோசமானதாக இருக்கலாம், நாம் நன்றாகவும் உண்மையாகவும் படுகுழியில் இருந்தாலும் கூட, விளைவுகளைப் பற்றி சிறிதும் யோசிக்காமல் மகிழ்ச்சியான வழியில் செல்லும் அரசியல் தலைமையின் விளைவு. ஆனால் தற்போதைய கொந்தளிப்பு தொடர்பாக சட்டத்தின் சமமற்ற செயல்பாடு குறித்து பிரதமரின் விளக்கத்திற்குத் திரும்புவது, இது வெறுக்கத்தக்கது மற்றும் தவறானது.

அரசாங்கம் சார்பு மற்றும் அரசாங்க எதிர்ப்பு ஆதரவாளர்களுக்கு எதிராக சட்டம் செயல்படும் சமநிலையின் ஆபத்தான பற்றாக்குறை தெளிவாக உள்ளது. மே மாதம் 9ஆம் திகதி தூண்டுதலின் பேரில் விதிக்கப்பட்ட பயணத்தடையை அடுத்து, முன்னாள் பிரதமர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷ மற்றும் இரண்டு முன்னாள் அமைச்சர்கள் உட்பட 'பலர்' இன்னும் தங்கள் கடவுச்சீட்டை நீதிமன்றத்தில் சமர்ப்பிக்கவில்லை என்று இந்த வாரம், சட்டமா அதிபர் திணைக்கள அதிகாரிகள் நீதிமன்றத்தில் சமர்பித்தனர். காலி முகத்திடலில் அமைதியான ஆர்ப்பாட்டக்காரர்கள் மீது தாக்குதல் முன்னாள் அமைச்சர் ஜோன்ஸ்டன் பெர்னாண்டோ சரணடைவதற்கு முன்னர் பல வாரங்களாக அவர்கள் சொல்வது போல் சட்டத்தின் ‘தெளிவான பார்வையில்’ ஒளிந்திருந்தார்.

மறுபுறம், இளைஞர் எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் தாராளமாக கைது செய்யப்படுகின்றனர் அல்லது மிரட்டல் நோக்குடன் காவல்துறையினரால் விசாரிக்கப்படுகிறார்கள். பிரதமரிடம் கேள்வி கேட்கும் இளம் பேட்டியாளர் கவனம் செலுத்திய சட்டத்தின் சமத்துவமின்மை அது.

அவள் பெற்ற பதில்களின் புத்திசாலித்தனமும் அழுத்தமும் அந்த அப்பட்டமான உண்மையிலிருந்து விலகவில்லை. உண்மையில், இந்த பாரபட்சம் மற்றும் பாரபட்சம், எதிர்ப்பாளர்களை கைது செய்வதில் பிரதிபலிக்கும் சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கத்தின் சமீபத்திய கடிதம், காவல் கண்காணிப்பாளர் (IGP) க்கு அனுப்பிய கடிதத்தில் சுட்டிக்காட்டப்பட்டது. அரசியல்வாதிகள்.

பாரபட்சம் குறித்த மற்ற குற்றச்சாட்டுகளில், நபர்களை காவலில் வைக்க, காலாவதியான அவசரகாலச் சட்டங்களைப் பயன்படுத்தியது. காவல்துறைக்கான அரசியல் நியமனங்களால் காவல்துறையின் கட்டளைக் கட்டமைப்பு சீர்குலைந்துள்ளது என்று ஐஜிபியே ஒப்புக்கொண்டது சரியாகவே வலியுறுத்தப்பட்டது. அந்த சமத்துவமின்மை பல்வேறு நிலைகளில் தொடர்ந்து வெளிப்பட்டுக் கொண்டே இருக்கிறது.
வர்த்தகர் ஒருவரை மிரட்டி பணம் பறித்த குற்றச்சாட்டில் முன்னாள் அமைச்சர் பிரசன்ன ரணதுங்க குற்றவாளி என சர்வதேச செய்தி ஊடகங்களில் திங்கட்கிழமை முன்னணி தலைப்புச் செய்திகள் அறிவித்தன.

இதைப் பற்றிய இரண்டு அதிர்ச்சியூட்டும் செய்திகளுக்கு, 'இலங்கையின் நகர்ப்புற மேம்பாட்டு அமைச்சர் மிரட்டி பணம் பறித்த வழக்கில் தண்டனை பெற்றுள்ளார்', ஸ்ட்ரெய்ட்ஸ் டைம்ஸ், ஜூன் 6 மற்றும் 'இலங்கை கிரிக்கெட் வீரர் ரணதுங்காவின் சகோதரருக்கு மிரட்டி பணம் பறித்ததற்காக இரண்டு ஆண்டுகள் சிறைத்தண்டனை' ஆகியவற்றைப் பார்க்கவும். அதே தேதியில்).

அதற்கேற்ற வகையில், இந்தத் தண்டனை இந்த தகுதியானவரின் பாராளுமன்ற ஆசனத்தையோ அல்லது அவரது அமைச்சர் பதவியையோ பாதிக்காது என்பதற்கு அரசாங்கம் விரைவான பதிலைக் கொண்டுள்ளது. உண்மைதான், அரசியலமைப்பின் 89வது பிரிவுடன் வாசிக்கப்பட்ட 91வது சட்டப்பிரிவு, அதன் சட்ட வரம்புக்குள், இடைநீக்கம் செய்யப்பட்ட தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்ட நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர்களை உள்ளடக்காது.

தற்செயலாக, நாடாளுமன்றத்தில் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்படும்/பணியாற்றுவதற்கான தகுதி நீக்கம் தண்டனையை அனுபவிக்க உத்தரவிடப்பட்டவர்களுக்கு மட்டுமே பொருந்தும் என்ற நிபந்தனையை நீக்குவதற்கு, இந்த அரசியலமைப்பு விதிகளில் திருத்தம் செய்ய வேண்டிய நேரம் இதுவாக இருக்கலாம்.

இது தவிர, ஒரு குற்றவாளிக்கு இடைநீக்கம் செய்யப்பட்ட தண்டனையை வழங்குவதற்கான சட்டப்பூர்வ நியாயமானது, குற்றவியல் நடத்தை மீண்டும் நடக்காது என்ற மதிப்பீட்டிற்கு செல்கிறது. இது நம் அரசியல்வாதிகளுக்கு கொடுக்கப்பட்டதா, நாம் கேட்கலாம்?
பொருட்படுத்தாமல், சட்டத்தை விடவும், பிரச்சினை என்னவென்றால், ஒரு குற்றவாளி பாராளுமன்றத்தில் இருப்பது மட்டுமல்லாமல் அவரது இலாகாவை வைத்திருப்பதை அரசாங்கம் வரவேற்கும் சட்ஜ்பாதான்.

ஆகவே, ஜனாதிபதியும் பிரதமரும் அரசாங்கத்தை முழுவதுமாக தங்கள் சொந்த செயலில் இருந்து மீட்டெடுக்க பணம் கொடுங்கள் என்று உலக நாடுகளிடம் கெஞ்சும்போது, ​​அதில் உள்ள பெரிய முரண்பாட்டை அவர்கள் அங்கீகரிக்கவில்லையா?
அல்லது ஒருவேளை நடப்பது போல், அவர்கள் அரசியல் நாணயத்தின் மேலும் ஒரு டாஸில் பிச்சை எடுக்கிறார்களா?

அப்படியானால், உலகக் கருத்து இந்த வளர்ச்சிகளை நிச்சயமற்ற வகையில் இணைக்கிறது என்பதை அவர்கள் அறிந்திருக்க வேண்டும். உதாரணமாக, பல உலகளாவிய செய்தி அறிக்கைகள் இந்த அமைச்சரின் தண்டனையை நாட்டின் நிதிக் கரைப்பின் பின்னணியில் பார்த்தன. இதை அரசியல் தலைமை புரிந்து கொள்ள நாம் இன்னும் எவ்வளவு முயல் குழியில் விழ வேண்டும்?

குறைந்தபட்சம், (அமைச்சர்) குற்றவாளியின் இலாகாவையாவது பறிக்க வேண்டாமா? தண்டனைக்கு எதிராக அவர் மேல்முறையீடு செய்திருப்பது ஒரு துளியும் வித்தியாசத்தை ஏற்படுத்தாது. உயர்ந்த இடங்களில் முரடர்களும், தாழ்வான இடங்களில் உள்ள முரட்டுக் காரர்களும் இலங்கையை ஏமாற்றிக்கொண்டே இருக்கிறார்கள். சமீபத்தியது, குழப்பமான பொது சர்ச்சைகளுக்குப் பெயர் பெற்ற நிதி ‘ஹை ஃப்ளையர்’ ஆகும், ஆனால் பதிவு செய்யப்பட்ட விரைவான நேரத்தில் தேசியப் பட்டியல் மூலம் நாடாளுமன்றத்தில் ‘பாராசூட்’ செய்யப்படுவதற்கு ஒப்புதல் அளிக்கப்பட்டது. ‘சிஸ்டம் மாற்றத்துக்காக’ இளைஞர்கள் நடத்தும் ஒரு மாதப் போராட்டத்திற்கு அரசு தலைகுனிவாக அணிவகுப்பது இதுதானா?

அரசியல் ஸ்தாபனம் தன்னை ஒருங்கிணைத்துக்கொண்டிருக்கும் வேளையில், இன்னொரு கும்பலுக்காக மந்திரி கோஷ்டிகளை மாற்றியதே தவிர இந்த எதிர்ப்புகள் தாக்கத்தை ஏற்படுத்தவில்லை. நிறுவன சுதந்திரத்தின் அடிப்படை அடித்தளங்கள் கூட நிராகரிக்கப்படுகின்றன. இலங்கை மத்திய வங்கியின் (CBSL) ஆளுநரிடம் ஜனாதிபதி ராஜபக்ஷ சில வாரங்களுக்கு முன்னர் CBSL உடன் தலையிட மாட்டேன் என்று உறுதியளித்தது ஒரு சிறந்த உதாரணம்.

ஆனால் CBSL மற்றும் நாணய வாரியத்தின் சுயாட்சி மற்றும் சுதந்திரம் ஜனாதிபதியின் பெருந்தன்மையின் மீது வழங்கப்பட வேண்டிய பரிசு அல்ல. அனைத்து நிறுவனங்களைப் போலவே, சுதந்திரம் சட்டத்தால் பாதுகாக்கப்பட வேண்டும், மேலும் நேர்மை மற்றும் நிபுணத்துவம் கொண்ட ஆண்கள் மற்றும் பெண்களால் நடைமுறையில் செயல்பட வேண்டும். அந்த விடயத்தில் இலங்கை வியக்கத்தக்க வகையில் தோல்வியடைந்துள்ளது. இந்தப் பதவிகளில் இருக்கும் அரசியல் கையாட்கள் இலங்கையை இப்போது நமக்குள்ள நெருக்கடிக்கு இட்டுச் சென்றுள்ளனர்.

en.title: Protests by the youth for ‘system change  have not had much impact  so far except for the changing of one set of  ministerial crooks for another set of crooks while the political establishment consolidates itself!
body_lines: When questioned as to why Sri Lanka’s law works to its most ferocious extent against anti-government protestors, Premier Ranil Wickremesinghe’s answer was typically slick.

‘The police have also arrested Members of Parliament…it is the magistrates who decide on remand’, he said. This was during one of multiple regional and global media interviews indulged in freely as the nation’s citizenry struggles in the death throes of decades of gross unchecked political corruption by Presidents and Prime Ministers of all shades and sizes.

Meanwhile allegations abound that citizens who have not committed violence are being hauled in for questioning, the latest being a Catholic nun. Premier Wickremesinghe peppered his remarks with references to the Bar Association’s role in working with state agencies to prevent abuse.

In passing, it must also be said that the Prime Minister’s co-opting of the Bar’s statements to bolster ‘his’ Government’s various positions, including the departure of the President which is a core demand of the protestors, is becoming uncomfortably frequent, to say the least.

The Bar’s denial, through a statement issued by its Executive Committee on Thursday, of the Prime Minister’s reported assertion that it ‘agreed’ to a quid-pro-quo with President Gotabhaya Rajapaksa that he could remain if the Executive Presidency is abolished, is a case in point.

The President himself asserted recently that he will continue in office and that he cannot go out as a ‘failed President.’ This testifies to the fact that the pressure which erupted as a result of the country-wide protests have now been, in  the Government’s thinking at least, ‘managed’ with the well planned advent of Premier Wickremesinghe. The President and the Prime Minister may both be nastily wrong in that assessment, despite a present lull as it were. From village to city, Sri Lankans are growing increasingly desperate as families starve.

With desperation comes fury as evidenced on May 9th apart from the fact that some attacks on offices and residences of politicians were well orchestrated by intra-party rivalries and other hidden sinister hands. Renewed violence may well be far worse than this, the result of a political leadership that goes on its merry way with scarce a thought for the consequences, even when we are well and truly in the abyss.  But to return to the Prime Ministerial explanation in regard to the unequal working of the law in regard to the present turmoil, this is both disingenuous and plainly incorrect.

The alarming lack of balance with which the law operates against pro-Government and anti-Government supporters is clear. This week, officers of the Attorney General’s Department submitted to court that former Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa and ‘several others’ including two former Ministers have not yet submitted their passports to court following a travel ban imposed on them in connection with instigation of the May 9th assaults on peaceful protestors at Galle face Green.  Former Minister Johnston Fernando was hiding in ‘plain sight’ of the law as they say, for several weeks before he surrendered.

On the other hand, youth protestors are liberally arrested or questioned by the police with a view to intimidation. That is the inequality of the law that the young interviewer questioning the Prime Minister was focussing on.

The clever parry and thrust of the answers that she received did not detract from that stark truth. In fact, this partiality and discrimination reflected in police arrests of protestors was pointed to in a recent letter of the Bar Association addressed to the Inspector General of Police (IGP), drawing serious concern to arrests of citizens being carried out solely on lists submitted by politicians.

Among other allegations of partiality was the use of lapsed Emergency Regulations to keep persons in custody. Rightly, the admission by the IGP himself that the command structure of the police has broken down due to political appointments to the police force, was emphasized. That inequality continues to be manifested at various other levels.
On Monday leading headlines in international news media announced the conviction of former Minister Prasanna Ranatunga on charges of extorting money from a businessman.

For two coruscating news reports on this, see ‘Sri Lanka’s urban development minister convicted of extortion’, Straits Times, June 6th and ‘Sri Lankan cricketer Ranatunga’s brother sentenced to two years imprisonment for extortion’ Times of India carrying a Press Trust of India story of the same date).

Commensurate with that, we have the Government quick-fire response to the effect that the conviction will not affect this worthy’s parliamentary seat or his ministerial portfolio. True, Article 91 read with Article 89 of the Constitution does not encompass parliamentarians on whom suspended sentences have been imposed, within its legal remit.

Incidentally, it may be high time that we look at amendment of these constitutional provisions to take out the condition that the disqualification to be elected/serve in Parliament applies only to those who are ordered to serve the sentence.

Quite apart from that, the legal rationale for imposing a suspended sentence on an offender goes to an assessment that criminal behaviour will not be repeated. Is that a given with our politicians, we may well ask?
Regardless, even more than the law, the issue is the chutzpah with which the Government greets the fact that a criminal continues not only to be in Parliament but holds his portfolio.
So when the President and the Prime Minister beg the world to give money to bail the Government out of the mess that is wholly their own doing, do they not recognize the grand irony thereto?
Or as is probably the case, do they simply stake their begging on one more toss of the political coin?

If so, they must be educated that world opinion links these developments in no uncertain terms. For example, many global news reports looked at the conviction of this Minister in the context of the country’s financial meltdown. How much more do we have to fall down the Rabbit Hole for the political leadership to understand this?

At least, should not the (Ministerial) convict be stripped of his portfolio? That he has appealed against the conviction does not make an iota of a difference. Rogues at high places and rogues at low places continues to bedevil Sri Lanka. The latest is a financial ‘high flyer’ known for messy public controversies but approved to be ‘parachuted’ to Parliament through the National List in record quick time. Is this what the Government parades as a nod for month long protests by the youth for ‘system change’?

These protests have not had impact except for the changing of ministerial crooks for another set of crooks while the political establishment consolidates itself. Even basic foundations of institutional independence are discarded. A classic example was when President Rajapaksa assured the Governor of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) some weeks ago that he will not ‘interfere’ with the CBSL.

But the autonomy and independence of the CBSL and the Monetary Board is not a gift to be granted on Presidential magnanimity. As with all institutions, independence must be secured by law as well as acted upon in practice by men and women of integrity and expertise. Sri Lanka has spectacularly failed in that regard. Political sycophants in these positions have led Sri Lanka to the crisis that is now upon us.
